docs.title: bitalino-rs
docs.text: Rust driver with Python bindings for BITalino biosignal acquisition over Bluetooth RFCOMM (BlueZ, no root required).

docs.title: What this library does
docs.text:
• Discovers/pairs with a BITalino via MAC address, then opens an RFCOMM stream.
  • Streams frames at 1/10/100/1000 Hz with CRC checks and sequence counters to flag gaps.
  • Exposes the same concepts in Rust and Python: Bitalino, Frame, FrameBatch, DeviceState.
  • Provides timing hints (microsecond timestamps) so you can rebuild sample times on the host side.

docs.title: How the pieces fit
docs.text: 1) Connect: use the Bluetooth connector to pair and open RFCOMM. 2) Start: select sampling rate and channel mask; device begins streaming immediately. 3) Read: pull batches; inspect CRC and sequence gaps to detect drops. 4) Stop: stop streaming and close the transport cleanly.

docs.title: Quick start (Python)
docs.text:
import bitalino_rs as brs

device = brs.Bitalino.connect("7E:91:2B:C4:AF:08")
device.start(rate=1000, channels=[0, 1, 2])
batch = device.read(200)
print(f"frames={len(batch)}, crc_errors={batch.crc_errors}, gaps={batch.sequence_gaps}")
device.stop()

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
• Install (see installation).
  • Explore the Python API reference (python_api).
  • Dive into driver internals in the Rust docs (rust_api).

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
uv sync --group dev         # install Python tooling
uv run maturin develop

maturin develop builds and installs the Rust extension into the uv environment so mkdocstrings can import bitalino_rs.

docs.title: Run tests
docs.text: uv run cargo test --all

docs.title: Build docs locally
docs.text:
cargo doc --no-deps --all-features
mkdir -p docs/rust-docs
cp -r target/doc/* docs/rust-docs/
echo "*" > docs/rust-docs/.gitignore  # ignore generated files
uv run mkdocs serve

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.11 or newer
  • Rust toolchain with cargo
  • Bluetooth adapter with BlueZ on Linux (required for RFCOMM access)

docs.title: Install from PyPI
docs.text: uv add bitalino-rs

docs.title: Develop from source (uv-first)
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/tleygue/bitalino_rs.git
cd bitalino_rs
uv sync          # installs Python tooling declared in pyproject

docs.title: Python API
docs.text:
Autogenerated reference for the Python bindings exposed by bitalino_rs.

Key objects you will see here:

  • Bitalino: connect, start/stop streaming, read frames/batches, query device state.
  • Frame / FrameBatch: data containers with CRC and sequence-gap metadata.
  • DeviceState: on-demand snapshot for BITalino 2.0+ devices.

BITalino Rust driver with Python bindings.

This package provides a robust interface to BITalino biosignal acquisition devices via Bluetooth RFCOMM, with automatic pairing and no root privileges required.

Quick Start

import bitalino_rs device = bitalino_rs.Bitalino.connect("98:D3:51:FE:6F:A3") print(f"Firmware: {device.version()}")

Classes:

Name Description Bitalino

Main device driver class.

Frame

A single data frame with sequence, digital, and analog values.

FrameBatch

Batch of frames with timing and error statistics.

DeviceState

Device state information (BITalino 2.0+ only).

Constants

DEFAULT_SAMPLING_RATE: Default sampling rate (1000 Hz). VALID_SAMPLING_RATES: List of valid rates [1, 10, 100, 1000].

docs.title: Check battery (BITalino 2.0+ only)
docs.text:
if device.is_bitalino2: ... state = device.state() ... print(f"Battery: {state.battery_voltage:.2f}V")

device.start(rate=1000, channels=[0, 1, 2]) frames = device.read(100) device.stop()

docs.title: Bitalino
docs.text:
BITalino device driver.

Provides an interface to BITalino biosignal acquisition devices via Bluetooth RFCOMM or serial connections. Supports automatic pairing without root privileges.

Attributes:

Name Type Description sampling_rate int

Currently configured sampling rate in Hz.

elapsed_us int | None

Microseconds since acquisition started (None if not started).

is_bitalino2 bool

True if device is BITalino 2.0+ (supports extended features).

Example

device = Bitalino.connect("7E:91:2B:C4:AF:08") print(f"Firmware: {device.version()}") print(f"BITalino 2.0: {device.is_bitalino2}")

docs.title: Check battery state (BITalino 2.0+ only)
docs.text:
if device.is_bitalino2: ... state = device.state() ... print(f"Battery: {state.battery_voltage:.2f}V")

device.start(rate=1000, channels=[0, 1, 2]) batch = device.read_timed(1000) print(f"Got {len(batch)} frames, {batch.crc_errors} errors") device.stop()

docs.title: battery(value: int = 30) -> None
docs.text:
Alias for set_battery_threshold() for compatibility with official library.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default value int

Threshold value (0-63). Default: 30.

30

Raises:

Type Description RuntimeError

If device is currently in acquisition mode

docs.title: connect(address: str, pin: str = '1234') -> Bitalino staticmethod
docs.text:
Connect to a BITalino device via Bluetooth.

Automatically discovers the device, pairs with the given PIN, and establishes an RFCOMM connection. No root privileges required. Includes automatic retry logic for flaky connections.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default address str

Bluetooth MAC address (e.g., "7E:91:2B:C4:AF:08")

required pin str

Pairing PIN code (default: "1234")

'1234'

Returns:

Type Description Bitalino

A connected Bitalino instance

Raises:

Type Description ConnectionError

If the device cannot be found, paired, or connected

docs.title: pwm(value: int = 100) -> None
docs.text:
Set PWM output value (BITalino 2.0+ only).

Controls the pulse-width modulation output for LED brightness or other PWM-driven devices.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default value int

PWM duty cycle (0-255). 0 = 0% duty cycle (always off) 255 = 100% duty cycle (always on) Default: 100 (~39% duty cycle)

100

Raises:

Type Description RuntimeError

If device is not BITalino 2.0+

docs.title: read(n_frames: int = 100) -> list[Frame]
docs.text:
Read frames from the device.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default n_frames int

Number of frames to read. Default: 100.

100

Returns:

Type Description list[Frame]

List of Frame objects containing the acquired data.

list[Frame]

May contain fewer frames than requested if CRC errors occur.

Raises:

Type Description IOError

If reading fails

docs.title: read_timed(n_frames: int = 100) -> FrameBatch
docs.text:
Read frames with timing and error information.

This is the recommended method for high-quality acquisition as it provides timing information for sample reconstruction and error statistics.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default n_frames int

Number of frames to read. Default: 100.

100

Returns:

Type Description FrameBatch

FrameBatch with frames, timestamp_us, crc_errors, and sequence_gaps.

Raises:

Type Description IOError

If reading fails

docs.title: set_battery_threshold(threshold: int = 30) -> None
docs.text:
Set the battery threshold level.

When battery voltage drops below this threshold, the device LED will blink. Must be called when not in acquisition mode.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default threshold int

Threshold value (0-63). 0 = 3.4V (minimum), 63 = 3.8V (maximum). Default: 30 (~3.6V)

30

Raises:

Type Description RuntimeError

If device is currently in acquisition mode

docs.title: start(rate: int = 1000, channels: list[int] | None = None) -> None
docs.text:
Start data acquisition.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default rate int

Sampling rate in Hz. Must be 1, 10, 100, or 1000. Default: 1000.

1000 channels list[int] | None

List of analog channels to acquire (0-5). Default: all channels.

None

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If rate or channels are invalid

RuntimeError

If the device fails to start

docs.title: state() -> DeviceState
docs.text:
Get the current device state (BITalino 2.0+ only).

Returns the current values of all analog channels, digital channels, battery level, and battery threshold. Must be called when not in acquisition.

Returns:

Type Description DeviceState

DeviceState object with analog, battery, battery_threshold, digital,

DeviceState

battery_voltage, and is_battery_low properties.

Raises:

Type Description RuntimeError

If device is not BITalino 2.0+ or in acquisition mode

IOError

If communication fails or CRC error

docs.title: stop() -> None
docs.text:
Stop data acquisition.

Raises:

Type Description RuntimeError

If the device fails to stop

docs.title: trigger(outputs: list[int] | None = None) -> None
docs.text:
Set digital output pins.

Controls the digital output pins for external circuits or LED control.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default outputs list[int] | None

List of output values (0 or 1). BITalino 2.0: [O1, O2] - works in both idle and acquisition modes BITalino 1.0: [O1, O2, O3, O4] - requires acquisition mode Default: [0, 0]

None

Raises:

Type Description RuntimeError

If BITalino 1.0 and not in acquisition mode

docs.title: version() -> str
docs.text:
Get the BITalino firmware version string.

Also detects if device is BITalino 2.0+ (sets is_bitalino2 property).

Returns:

Type Description str

Firmware version (e.g., "BITalino_v5.2")

Raises:

Type Description IOError

If communication fails

docs.title: DeviceState
docs.text:
Device state information (BITalino 2.0+ only).

Contains current values of all analog/digital channels and battery status. Obtained by calling Bitalino.state() when not in acquisition mode.

Attributes:

Name Type Description analog list[int]

All 6 analog channel values (10-bit, 0-1023).

battery int

Battery ADC value (10-bit, 0-1023).

battery_threshold int

Current battery threshold setting (0-63).

digital list[int]

Digital channel states [I1, I2, O1, O2].

battery_voltage float

Approximate battery voltage in Volts.

is_battery_low bool

True if battery is below threshold setting.

docs.title: to_dict() -> dict[str, Any]
docs.text: Convert to dictionary for easy serialization.

docs.title: Frame
docs.text:
A single BITalino data frame (immutable, hashable).

Attributes:

Name Type Description sequence int

Frame sequence number (0-15, wrapping). Use to detect dropped frames.

digital list[int]

Digital channel values [I1, I2, O1, O2] as list of 0/1.

analog list[int]

Analog channel values (10-bit, 0-1023).

n_channels int

Number of analog channels in this frame.

docs.title: to_dict() -> dict[str, Any]
docs.text: Convert to dictionary for easy serialization.

docs.title: FrameBatch
docs.text:
Result from reading a batch of frames, includes timing and error info.

Attributes:

Name Type Description frames list[Frame]

List of Frame objects.

timestamp_us int

Microseconds since acquisition started when batch was read.

crc_errors int

Number of frames discarded due to CRC errors.

sequence_gaps int

Number of detected dropped frames (from sequence discontinuities).

has_errors bool

True if any CRC errors or sequence gaps occurred.

docs.title: Rust API
docs.text:
The Rust documentation is generated with cargo doc and published alongside the site.

👉 Open Rust API documentation

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
To regenerate locally:

uv run cargo doc --no-deps --all-features

The HTML will land in target/doc/; copy target/doc/bitalino_rs to docs/rust-docs/bitalino_rs when publishing.
